'''Kuripitone "Tony" Tatupu''' is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played as a {{rlp|ce}} and in the {{rlp|sr}} and represented both [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Western Samoa]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Tatupu is the uncle of the rugby league footballer; [[Matthew Wright (rugby league)|Matthew Wright]].

==Playing career==
Tatupu first played for the [[Mt Albert Lions]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League|Auckland competition]] before joining the [[North Harbour Sea Eagles]] for the 1994 [[Lion Red Cup]] season. Between 1991 and 1993 he played 12 games for [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]].<ref>Richard Becht. ''A New Breed Rising: The Warriors Winfield Cup Challenge''. Auckland, HarperCollins, 1994. {{ISBN|1-86950-154-3}}. p.183</ref> From there he joined the [[Auckland Warriors]] for their inaugural season in the [[Australian Rugby League]] competition. Along with [[Nigel Vagana]] he joined the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] competition in 1997 before returning to the Warriors for another two seasons.

In 2000 he returned to England to finish his career at the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He stayed with the club during a time of financial turbulence mid-way through the 2000 season which saw all players aged over 24 released and then re-signed on limited wages.

==Representative career==
Tatupu represented the [[Kiwis (rugby league)|Kiwis]] tour of PNG 1994 in 4 tour matches + was capped with 4 Official test matches - Samoa at two [[Rugby League World Cup|World Cups]], and two [[Pacific Cup]]s. He was named in the [[1992 Pacific Cup (rugby league)|1992 Pacific Cup]]s team of the tournament.

==Later years==
He now serves as a New Zealand Police officer and represented the [[New Zealand Police]] at the [[Festival of World Cups|inaugural Police World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis hold off gallant Aussie police outfite">{{cite web|url=http://www.finda.com.au/story/2008/11/13/kiwis-hold-off-gallant-aussie-police-outfit|title=Kiwis hold off gallant Aussie police outfit|publisher=finda.com.au|date=13 November 2008|accessdate=1 January 2009|url-status=bot: unknown|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110706104028/http://www.finda.com.au/story/2008/11/13/kiwis-hold-off-gallant-aussie-police-outfit/|archivedate=6 July 2011}}</ref><ref name="Getting it on - Station of Origin">{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/288/getting-it-on---station-of-origi|title=Getting it on - Station of Origin|publisher=warriors.co.nz|date=13 July 2009|accessdate=1 January 2010|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20090726183010/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/288/getting-it-on---station-of-origi/|archivedate=26 July 2009}}</ref>

Tatupu's son Shem has been signed by [[Australian Football League]] (AFL) club [[Hawthorn Football Club|Hawthorn]] as an international rookie.<ref>{{cite web|last=Worthington|first=Sam|title=Wayne Schwass tips Kiwis to make AFL impact|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/other-sports/8593646/Wayne-Schwass-tips-Kiwis-to-make-AFL-impact|work=Stuff.co.nz|date=24 April 2013 |publisher=Fairfax Media|accessdate=12 May 2013}}</ref>
